pre_registration.question: Does R (using cosine E) correlate with domain-appropriate quality metrics across different data types, and does R outperform E alone?
pre_registration.reframe_note: v2 uses a single E definition (mean pairwise cosine sim) for all domains. This test does NOT change E per domain. The question is whether cosine-based R is a useful quality statistic beyond E alone.
pre_registration.v4_fix: DOMAIN-01 from AUDIT_v3: text_alt replaced with AG News, a genuinely different text corpus (4-class news: World, Sports, Business, Sci/Tech) instead of 20 Newsgroups with a different random seed.

decision_rule.CONFIRMED: R significantly outperforms E (Steiger p<0.05) in >=2/3 domains
decision_rule.FALSIFIED: R does not significantly outperform E in any domain

pre_registration.text_agnews_ground_truth: cluster purity on AG News (genuinely different corpus)
pre_registration.financial_note: Sharpe ratio is algebraically related to mean cosine of return vectors (both measure return consistency). Using sector purity instead provides a genuinely independent ground truth.

text_agnews.dataset_note: AG News: 4-class news classification (World, Sports, Business, Sci/Tech). 120,000 training documents. This is a GENUINELY DIFFERENT corpus from 20 Newsgroups (DOMAIN-01 fix from AUDIT_v3).

adjudication.decision_applied: CONFIRMED requires 2/3 sig beats. FALSIFIED requires 0/3 sig beats. Got 1/3.
